rule: never stage; seek and stage the original instead; log drop as inadmissible-origin
note: Most prolific CAR digital-news hit in every French query of the CAF sweep. Verified verbatim mirror of Oubangui Medias (AIAL data-centre story, reposted ~2 min after the original). High-volume, on-topic, correctly dated and superficially clean, so a naive FR run would build a country's record out of it. See ISSUE-013.

rule: never stage; never ingest; if the underlying event is real seek the genuine primary and stage that
note: Established fabricated by lint #6 on 2026-07-20. Its 2026-07-05 'Google Confirms R2 Billion AI Hub in South Africa' invented a R2.1bn Midrand AI campus, a Pretoria MoU with 60%-local-hiring and data-localisation conditions, SEZ tax concessions, an FFC forgone-tax estimate, job numbers, and two named officials who do not exist ('Google Africa director Nitin Gajjar'; 'FFC chair Dr. Patience Mlambo'). Google's actual Jul-2026 SA announcement was an Eastern Cape Digital Exchange Port with no disclosed value. Dangerous rather than merely weak: fluent, correctly dated, on-topic and specific enough to compile straight onto a page — which it was, onto south-africa--infra-store. Markers: served body is site chrome plus a teaser truncated at an ellipsis; bylines appear only in search indexes; headlines append an economic-stakes clause to unrelated stories ('Zimbabwe Challenges Bangladesh in Series — Economic Stakes High').

network: Low-authority aggregator (unattributed rewrite with ad injection)
rule: never stage; identify and stage the primary the item is rewritten from
note: ADJUDICATED 2026-07-20 (lint #6). Confirmed stays on the list. Kenyan host carrying Nigeria and DRC stories; stored bodies open with injected ad chrome ('Ad • Kings Lotus Ongata Rongai', 'Ad • Kings Summit') inside the verbatim capture. Only TWO of the three flagged raw/ files were actually its: the third (Mzalendo tracker) is Mzalendo's own site and merely contains an outbound streamlinefeed hyperlink inside its verbatim body — legitimate, left in place. Both streamlinefeed files retired and deleted. Not fabricated in the southafricanews24 sense: both underlying events are real and were confirmed against primaries. The defect is unattributed rewriting that adds invented colour and drifts the framing — it restyled a routine ministerial courtesy visit as a 'strategic security briefing', asserted NIMC 'bypassed traditional urban registration centers' and that enrolment officers 'will be accompanied by federal security personnel' (neither in any primary), reframed refugee inclusion as 'national security tracking', and appended an editorial Nigeria-vs-Kenya Maisha Namba contrast carried by no primary — all of which had compiled onto four wiki pages. It also mis-dated the event: it presented a drive that began 2026-02-16 as launched on its own publication date, exactly the failure CLAUDE.md's event-date rule guards against. Replaced by [2026-07-09 Nigerian Government Intensifies National Identity Enrolment Campaign (Voice of Nigeria)] and [2026-07-09 NIMC Enrols 136 Million Nigerians as Coker-Odusote Pushes Stronger Inter-Ministerial Partnership]; the DRC stablecoin item was already superseded by the REV-006 primaries and was struck without replacement.

rule: do not stage; the underlying event is usually covered better elsewhere
note: Flagged by lint #6 2026-07-20. Stored capture is a truncated excerpt block prefixed 'Africazine:', not a verbatim body, filed as body_completeness: full. Its held eKash item is superseded by better sources the wiki already holds (ITWeb, WeAreTech, New Times).
